name: Kumamoto Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the deep red-brown of Mount Aso's volcanic earth and the warrior heritage of the former Higo Province
the prefectural emblem; purity and peace

description: A deep maroon field charged at the centre with a white circular prefectural emblem stylising the katakana ク and マ of 'Kumamoto'.
history: Kumamoto's prefectural symbol was adopted on 1 July 1966; the design integrates the katakana ク and マ of 'Kumamoto' into a roughly circular form. The flag places the white emblem on a deep red-brown field.

features.symbolism: Mount Aso's volcanic earth and the historic warrior heritage of Higo (old Kumamoto)
a stylised graphic combining the katakana ク (ku) and マ (ma) for 'Kumamoto', forming a circular emblem evoking peace, harmony and the prefecture's outward-looking spirit
